architecture.summary: xRAG compresses retrieved documents into a single token by treating document embeddings from dense retrieval as features from a 'retrieval modality'. A trainable two-layer MLP projector (modality bridge) maps these frozen document embeddings into the LLM's representation space, replacing the full document text. The retriever and LLM remain frozen. Information flow: query → retrieve top-1 document → embed document with frozen retriever → project embedding through trainable MLP → inject as single token into LLM alongside query.

biblio.title: xRAG: Extreme Context Compression for Retrieval-augmented Generation with One Token

claims.dataset: NQ, TriviaQA, WebQA, HotpotQA, TruthfulQA, FactKG
claims.statement: xRAG achieves an average improvement of over 10% across six knowledge-intensive tasks

claims.dataset: FactKG, NQ, TriviaQA, WebQA (4 datasets averaged)
claims.statement: xRAG reduces overall FLOPs by a factor of 3.53 compared to uncompressed RAG model

claims.dataset: NQ, TriviaQA, WebQA, HotpotQA, TruthfulQA, FactKG
claims.statement: On Mistral-7b, xRAG achieves 16.2% relative improvement over non-retrieval baseline, compared to 19.4% for uncompressed RAG

claims.dataset: NQ, TriviaQA, WebQA, HotpotQA, TruthfulQA, FactKG
claims.statement: On Mixtral-8x7b, xRAG achieves 9.7% relative improvement over non-retrieval baseline, exceeding uncompressed RAG's 8.0%

claims.metric: resilience rate (% of instances correct before and after retrieval)
claims.statement: xRAG demonstrates higher resilience rate (82.3% on Mistral-7b, 84.9% on Mixtral-8x7b) compared to uncompressed RAG (75.2% average)
Document compression reduces token count from 175.1 to 1 token per document
xRAG lags behind uncompressed RAG in tasks requiring reasoning over documents, such as HotpotQA and FactKG
The projector MLP is the only trainable component, accounting for 0.46% of total parameters in Mistral-7b and 0.07% in Mixtral-8x7b
